initiatives.name: EVLovesNYC
initiatives.description: 501c3 non-profit founded by friends striving to end food insecurity in NYC!

initiatives.name: EV Neighbors Who Care
initiatives.description: Mutual aid in the East Village

initiatives.name: Sixth Street Center
initiatives.description: Sixth Street Community Center is a community-based not-for-profit organization founded in 1978 and committed to advancing social, environmental, housing, economic and racial justice in the East Village and Lower East Side community.

initiatives.name:
Community Care Collective
                                (ccc)
initiatives.description: Washington Sq Park, mutual aid, environmentalism, & liberation food distro: Tuesdays, 12:30-3:30

initiatives.name: WSP Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: Mutual Aid in Washington Square Park every Friday from 5 - 8 PM.

initiatives.name: Hell's Kitchen Community Cupboard
initiatives.description: Free food pantry in Hell's Kitchen 24/7. Mutual aid. Take what you need, leave what you can

initiatives.name: Hells Kitchen Free Store
initiatives.description: Lower waste stream. Waste diversion. neighbors helping neighbors. Give what you can. Take what you need.

initiatives.name: Anarchy 99
initiatives.description: On Sundays between 12-5pm the corner of E. 9th St and 1st Ave is a hub of radical art, mutual aid, community power and anarchist resistance.

initiatives.name: Art Against Displacement
initiatives.description: AAD supports fights against displacement. Based in NYC's Lower East Side & Chinatown on unceded Lenape land. We meet the 2nd Tuesday of every month.

initiatives.name: Bluestockings
initiatives.description: Queer, trans+SWer cooperatively-owned activist bookstore + café in NYC

initiatives.name: CAAAV: Organizing Asian Communities
initiatives.description: CAAAV organizes working class Asian immigrants in Chinatown and Astoria to make an intervention in the gentrification of our neighborhoods and New York City.

initiatives.name: Chinatown Art Brigade
initiatives.description: The Chinatown Art Brigade (CAB) is a cultural collective of artists, media makers and activists creating art and media to advance social justice.

initiatives.name:
Chinatown Community
                                Fridge
initiatives.description: By our community, for our community

initiatives.name: Chinatown Working Group
initiatives.description: The Chinatown Working Group is a broad coalition of community organizations with the common goal of protecting and preserving the culture and diversity of Chinatown and the Lower East Side from displacement and over-development, and of supporting the people who live and work among us.

initiatives.name: Feed the Streets NYC
initiatives.description: A hand up, not a hand out, 501c3 nonprofit Lower East Side, NYC

initiatives.name: Food Not Bombs Lower East Side
initiatives.description: Serving free vegan meals every Sunday at 4:30pm inside the SW corner of Tompkins Square Park (E 7th st & Ave A) DM us if you'd like to get involved!

initiatives.name: Tompkins Distro
initiatives.description: Saturday weekly community mutual aid distribution for migrant asylum seekers, unhoused neighbors & all in need at Tompkins Square Park. Come help!

initiatives.name:
Youth Against
                                Displacement
initiatives.description: Young and young at heart fighting displacement in the LES and Chinatown, picket with us every Thu-Sun 11am-1pm outside MOCA

initiatives.name: Friend of a Friend NYC
initiatives.description: Building networks of support with our unhoused friends & neighbors in Lower Manhattan.

initiatives.name: Midtown South Community Council
initiatives.description: Midtown South Community Council strives to dismantle the causes of homelessness by building an equitable, just, and sustainable social infrastructure.

initiatives.name: UES Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: Mutual aid network serving the Upper East Side community

initiatives.name: Defend Harlem
initiatives.description: Defend Harlem produces content, organizes public events, and connects with other organizations to raise community awareness about predatory development and create solutions to fight displacement.

initiatives.name: Fort Fridge
initiatives.description: Community Fridge in Washington Heights. Take what you need, give what you can. 181st St & Fort Washington Ave. Open 24/7

initiatives.name: Harlem 4 Palestine
initiatives.description: Fighting for liberation from Harlem to Palestine! DM us to get involved!

initiatives.name: The Harlem Community Fridge
initiatives.description: Community fridge at 250 Bradhurst Ave

initiatives.name: Indivisible Harlem
initiatives.description: fighting for progressive values in harlem

initiatives.name:
The People's Peoples
                                Fridge
initiatives.description: mutual aid group + fridge, 2113 Amsterdam Ave outside @wordupbooks

initiatives.name: Upper Manhattan Tenant Union
initiatives.description: Tenant-led group organizing in our bldgs & on our blocks to counter the violence of our landlords & fight for safe, stable, & dignified housing 4 all

initiatives.name: Uptown 4 Palestine
initiatives.description: Group of uptown Manhattan residents organizing for Palestinian liberation

initiatives.name: The Uptown Fridge
initiatives.description: Physical fridge location currently out of service. Weekly distributions mid-late Wed afternoons on Sherman between Thayer and Arden

initiatives.name: Uptown Wagon
initiatives.description: A collective of neighbors who meet in uptown Manhattan to distribute meals & supplies to neighbors experiencing homelessness.

initiatives.name: We the People
